scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Queue Backpressure and Consumer Lag
justification: All pass criteria met: throughput gap explicitly calculated (300/min vs ~18/min, 15:1 deficit), unbounded queue growth described with concrete projections (16,800 after 1hr, 134,400 after 8hrs), visibility timeout double-processing risk identified with arithmetic (10×3s≥30s), missing DLQ identified as a hard blocker, queue depth metric gap called out as 'the single most important metric', autoscaling to 15-20 instances recommended, and verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Throughput gap calculated precisely with concrete backlog projections over time
Visibility timeout arithmetic is detailed and correct — identifies that messages 8-10 in every batch will be redelivered
DLQ framed as a hard must-have blocker with specific maxReceiveCount recommendation
Queue depth metric gap identified as the most critical missing metric
Autoscaling recommendation is specific (15-20 instances) and alternatives offered (concurrency model)
Excellent escalation routing — capacity to MJ for architecture, idempotency to Bird for domain correctness
Goes beyond rubric requirements: identifies OOM risk, missing graceful shutdown, circuit breaker absence, and upstream flood risk
Structured must-haves vs nice-to-haves with clear prioritization
